QB-JDZ-EWR-012-ND  ·  ro-song-011

Qingbai Ewer with Carved Peonies

青白釉牡丹纹执壶 — Qīngbái Yòu Mǔdān Wén Zhíhú

Qingbai 青白瓷 Song Dynasty · 960–1279 CE Hutian Kilns, Jingdezhen Ewer · Zhíhú 執壺 Dronova Method Applied

Object Description

This elegant ewer represents one of the finest expressions of the Qingbai tradition (青白瓷, literally "blue-white ware"), produced at the Hutian kilns of Jingdezhen during the Song dynasty. The vessel combines a rounded, pear-shaped body with a slender waisted neck that flares into a wide trumpet mouth — a form known as zhíhú (執壺) — completed by a sweeping strap handle and an upward-angled pouring spout terminating level with the rim, a design feature characteristic of Northern Song production.

The exterior of the globular body is adorned with freely hand-carved peony scrolls executed with confident, fluid strokes directly into the leather-hard clay before glazing. Deeply incised stems divide the composition into swirling lobes, while petals are rendered in layered relief. A small applied floral boss marks the junction of spout and shoulder. The footring is wheel-trimmed and left unglazed, exposing the pale cream-buff body typical of Hutian kiln stonepaste.

The glaze is the defining feature: a thin, transparent calcium-rich fluid that settles with exceptional evenness across flat surfaces but accumulates in carved recesses, creating localised pools of intense blue-green. Viewed in raking light, the glaze exhibits the hallmark jade-like optical translucency that made Qingbai ware the most commercially successful ceramic export of the Song period.

Multi-component assembly: The ewer was constructed from at least four separately thrown and modelled parts — body, neck, spout and handle — luted together with slip before a single glaze application. Evidence of assembly seams is visible at the spout root and handle terminals under raking illumination.

Microscopic Analysis

Optical examination was conducted using reflected and transmitted illumination at magnifications of ×10 to ×100. Two zones were prioritised: the flat glaze surface of the shoulder (uninscribed zone, away from carved relief) and the handle and upper body area.

Zone A — Ручка и тулово, макро

Макро · ×4
×4 Macro

Зона корня ручки: непрерывность глазури через шов соединения. Кракле не обнаружено. Равномерная толщина глазури ок. 0,4–0,6 мм на плечике.

Ручка демонстрирует гладкую непрерывную поверхность глазури без следов кракле, отслоения или реставрационного перекрытия. При ×4 отчётливо выражено нефритоподобное оптическое качество — глазурь излучает холодное внутреннее свечение, характерное для стеклоподобных матриц, богатых кальцием.

Zone B — Плоская глазурь ×40

Оптический · ×40
×40

Почти однородная матрица с минимальной популяцией пузырьков. Редкие включения кварца (<0,05 мм). Окраска от следов оксидов железа и титана в кальциевой глазури.

При ×40 плоская глазурь демонстрирует характерную оптическую сигнатуру цинбай: почти однородная матрица с низкой концентрацией пузырьков и рассеянными включениями кварца. Это резко контрастирует с высокой концентрацией пузырьков у изделий Гуань и Жу. Приглушённый оливково-селадоновый тон в рассеянном свете отчётливо смещается в голубовато-зелёный при холодном северном освещении — главный хроматический критерий идентификации цинбай по Методу Дроновой.

Diagnostic Interpretation

Assessed against the full Dronova Method diagnostic matrix for Song-period Jingdezhen qingbai, this ewer satisfies all primary and secondary criteria for authentic Northern Song production at the Hutian kilns.

Diagnostic Parameter Observed Value Reference Standard (Hutian / Song) Assessment
Glaze colour (daylight)Pale bluish-green, slight grey castBluish-white to blue-green✓ Consistent
Glaze transparencyHigh — sub-glaze carving clearly visibleTransparent to translucent✓ Consistent
Microbubble populationLow — sparse, widely dispersedLow (cf. Guan: very high)✓ Diagnostic
Glaze pooling behaviourPronounced in carved channelsPools in recesses, thins on ridges✓ Consistent
Body colour (footring)Pale cream-buff, light iron specklingOff-white to cream paste✓ Consistent
Footring treatmentWheel-trimmed, unglazedUnglazed or lightly kiln-dusted✓ Consistent
Vessel constructionMulti-part assembly, slip-lutedAssembled from thrown components✓ Consistent
Decorative techniqueHand-carved sub-glazeCarved, combed or moulded✓ Consistent
Spout-rim alignmentSpout tip level with mouth rimNorthern Song convention✓ N. Song indicator
Applied boss at spout rootPresent — floral knopCommon in N. Song ewers✓ N. Song indicator

Diagnostic Checklist

  • Transparent calcium-rich glaze with characteristic bluish-green tone — primary qingbai identification criterion
  • Low microbubble density distinguishes from Guan, Ru and Jun wares produced contemporaneously
  • Glaze pooling in carved recesses creates chromatic modulation across decorative surface — authentic hand-carved behaviour
  • Pale cream-buff unglazed footring consistent with Hutian kiln stonepaste composition (high-kaolin, low-iron body)
  • Spout tip aligned with mouth rim — chronological marker for Northern Song rather than Southern Song production
  • Applied floral boss at spout-shoulder junction — decorative convention specific to N. Song Jingdezhen ewers
  • Multi-component assembly with slip luting confirmed at handle and spout terminals
  • Jade-like optical translucency — luminous depth without surface sheen excess — hallmark of authentic Song qingbai
  • No evidence of reglaze, later repair, artificial ageing or false patination under optical examination
Сочетание совмещения носика с краем устья, прикладного бутона и низкой концентрации микропузырьков составляет ключевую триаду атрибуции кувшинов цинбай печей Хутянь периода Северной Сун по диагностической системе Дроновой. Все три критерия удовлетворяются в данном образце.

Cross-References

ro-song-013 — Qingbai Meiping Vase

Shares identical glaze composition and low-microbubble matrix; meiping form illustrates the full range of Hutian kiln vessel production in the Northern Song period.

→ View ro-song-013

ro-song-014 — Qingbai Bowl with Moulded Decoration

Contrast between carved (this ewer) and moulded decoration techniques on qingbai ware. Both show glaze pooling behaviour; moulded relief produces shallower tonal gradient.

→ View ro-song-014

ro-song-007 — Guan Ware Brush Washer

Key comparative foil: Guan ware exhibits high microbubble population and thick opaque glaze in direct contrast to the low-bubble transparent matrix of this qingbai ewer.

→ View ro-song-007

Bibliography

Dronova, N. (2025). Optical and Microscopic Diagnostics of Song-Period Qingbai Ware: The Hutian Kiln Corpus. Global Archive of Antique Chinese Porcelain Authentication, Working Paper 11.

Kerr, R. & Wood, N. (2004). Science and Civilisation in China, Vol. 5, Part 12: Ceramic Technology. Cambridge University Press.

Pierson, S. (1996). Qingbai Ware: Chinese Porcelain of the Song and Yuan Dynasties. Percival David Foundation, London.

Liu, X. (2007). "Compositional analysis of Hutian kiln qingbai glazes." Journal of Archaeological Science 34(8), 1412–1424.

Wood, N. (1999). Chinese Glazes: Their Origins, Chemistry and Recreation. A&C Black / University of Pennsylvania Press.
